title: surveywts Package Development
text: Part surveyverse ecosystem. surveywts provides tools survey weighting calibration.

title: surveywts Package Development
text: Next action: Begin Phase 1. Start /spec-workflow draft spec. Full roadmap plans/roadmap.md.

title: surveywts Package Development
text: filled Phase 0 API designed. See plans/ specs. Error classes: surveywts_error_{snake_case_condition} Warning classes: surveywts_warning_{snake_case_condition} Internal helpers: prefix . (e.g., .validate_weights())

title: surveywts Package Development
text: Design variables sacred — never remove silently rename weight columns. Always warn weight column modified. Metadata lifecycle — metadata added later, auto-delete removal; auto-rename rename; track transformation history.

title: surveywts Package Development
text: Every non-trivial change lives feature branch — never commit implementation code main develop Branch naming: feature/, fix/, test/, docs/, chore/ commits use Conventional Commits format: feat(scope): description Valid scopes: classes, constructors, validators, weights, calibration, utils Run devtools::document() committing file roxygen2 changes Run devtools::check() opening PR

title: surveywts Package Development
text: Examples must load Imports packages explicitly. R CMD check runs examples fresh session library(surveywts) loaded. example calls bare function Imports package, add library(pkg) top block.

title: surveywts Package Development
text: plans/error-messages.md — canonical error/warning class names CLI message templates .claude/WORKFLOW.md — skills fit together (planning arc → implementation loop) .claude/rules/code-style.md — S7 patterns, cli errors, arg order, air formatter .claude/rules/r-package-conventions.md — imports, NAMESPACE, roxygen2, export policy .claude/rules/surveywts-conventions.md — package-specific naming, families, visibility .claude/rules/testing-standards.md — test structure, coverage targets, assertion patterns .claude/rules/testing-surveywts.md — package-specific test invariants data generators .claude/rules/github-strategy.md — branching model, commit format, versioning .claude/rules/engineering-preferences.md — DRY, edge cases, /-engineering

title: NA
text: Tests written passing (devtools::test()) R CMD check: 0 errors, 0 warnings (devtools::check()) Roxygen docs updated devtools::document() run plans/error-messages.md updated (new errors/warnings added) PR title valid Conventional Commit (feat(scope): description)

title: External Algorithm Delegation
text: file documents external packages surveywts delegates calibration algorithms . packages listed DESCRIPTION Imports.

title: External Algorithm Delegation
text: Functions used .calibrate_engine(): survey::calibrate() — GREG linear logit calibration survey::rake() — Iterative proportional fitting (IPF) survey::postStratify() — Post-stratification survey::svydesign() — Temporary design objects delegation survey::cal.linear, survey::cal.logit — Calibration function objects Numerical correctness verified direct survey:: calls within 1e-8 tolerance tests/testthat/test-02-calibrate.R, tests/testthat/test-03-rake.R, tests/testthat/test-04-poststratify.R.

title: External Algorithm Delegation
text: Functions used .calibrate_engine(): anesrake::anesrake() — IPF raking chi-square variable selection Notes: anesrake::anesrake() called force1 = FALSE preserve total weight (consistent survey::rake() behaviour). Convergence detected $converge character field: "Complete convergence achieved" "Results stable..." treated converged. variables already meet margins, anesrake::selecthighestpcts() throws error caught translated surveywts_message_already_calibrated message.

title: External Algorithm Delegation
text: adjust_nonresponse() function uses weighting-class nonresponse adjustment method. standard procedure survey statistics originate specific package. Implementation based closed-form formula: weighting class c, adjusted weight respondent class c : w_adjusted_i = w_i * (sum(w_j, j c) / sum(w_j, j c, j responded)) formula redistributes total weight non-respondents respondents within weighting class, preserving total weight. Correctness validated via hand-calculation tests/testthat/test-05-nonresponse.R.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jacob Dennen. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dennen J (2026). surveywts: Tools Survey Weighting Calibration. https://github.com/JDenn0514/surveywts.
code: @Manual{,   title = {{surveywts}: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration},   author = {Jacob Dennen},   year = {2026},   url = {https://github.com/JDenn0514/surveywts}, }

what: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration
title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration
text: surveywts provides tidy tools calibrating survey weights known population totals, adjusting nonresponse, diagnosing weight quality — full weighting history tracking reproducible survey analysis.

title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration
code: # From GitHub (development version) pak::pak("JDenn0514/surveywts")  # From r-universe (pre-built binaries, no GitHub PAT needed) install.packages("surveywts", repos = "https://jdenn0514.r-universe.dev")

title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration
text: surveywts part surveyverse ecosystem. provides three calibration methods, nonresponse adjustment, weight diagnostics — using tidy, formula-free syntax. Every function tracks full weighting history can audit exactly transformations applied order.

title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration
code: library(surveywts)

title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration
text: Full documentation available https://jdenn0514.github.io/surveywts/.

what: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
title: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
text: Redistributes weights nonrespondents respondents within weighting classes defined . rows (respondents nonrespondents) returned; nonrespondent weights set 0 respondent weights adjusted upward conserve total weight within cell.

title: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
code: adjust_nonresponse(   data,   response_status,   weights = NULL,   by = NULL,   method = c("weighting-class", "propensity-cell", "propensity"),   control = list(min_cell = 20, max_adjust = 2) )

title: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
text: data data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. Must include respondents nonrespondents. survey_replicate → error. class → error. response_status Bare name (NSE). Binary response indicator column. Must logical integer 0/1. 1 / TRUE = respondent. weights Bare name (NSE). Weight column. NULL → auto-detected weighted_df attribute survey object @variables$weights. plain data.frame weights = NULL, uniform starting weights used output column named ".weight". <tidy-select> Weighting class variables. Redistribution performed within cell defined joint combination variables. NULL → global redistribution across rows. method Character scalar. Adjustment method. Phase 0, "weighting-class" supported. "propensity" "propensity-cell" API-stable stubs error Phase 2. control Named list warning thresholds: min_cell: warn cell fewer many respondents (default 20, per NAEP methodology). max_adjust: warn nonresponse adjustment factor cell exceeds value (default 2.0, per survey::sparseCells() convention). Either condition alone triggers warning.

title: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
text: rows (respondents nonrespondents) returned. Nonrespondent weights set 0; respondent weights adjusted upward conserve total weight within cell. data.frame weighted_df input -> weighted_df survey_nonprob input -> survey_nonprob (class) survey_taylor input -> survey_taylor (class; respondent rows , survey_taylor support zero weights) history entry operation = "nonresponse_weighting_class" appended weighting_history.

title: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
text: adjustment formula within cell h : $$w_{,new} = w_i \times \frac{\sum w_h}{\sum w_{h,resp}}$$ \(\sum w_h\) sum weights (respondents + nonrespondents) cell h \(\sum w_{h,resp}\) sum respondent weights . Zero-weight observations retained design-based variance estimation. Survey estimation functions (e.g., survey::svymean()) handle zero weights correctly – zero-weight units excluded point estimates included design structure variance estimation. manual calculations, use w[w > 0] exclude nonrespondents. Diagnostic functions (effective_sample_size(), weight_variability(), summarize_weights()) automatically filter positive weights computing statistics. Re-calibrating post-nonresponse data requires filtering respondents first, calibrate(), rake(), poststratify() reject zero weights.

title: Adjust survey weights for unit nonresponse — adjust_nonresponse
code: df <- data.frame(   age_group = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+", "18-34", "35-54"),   responded = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 0L, 1L),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) result <- adjust_nonresponse(df, response_status = responded) #> Warning: ! Weighting class cell "(global)" is sparse (4 respondent(s), adjustment factor #>   1.25×). #> ℹ Small or high-adjustment cells may produce extreme weights. #> ℹ Consider collapsing weighting classes or adjusting `control$min_cell` / #>   `control$max_adjust`.

what: Calibrate survey weights to known population totals — calibrate
title: Calibrate survey weights to known population totals — calibrate
text: Adjusts survey weights weighted marginal totals match known population values. Supports linear (GREG) logit calibration methods categorical auxiliary variables.

title: Calibrate survey weights to known population totals — calibrate
code: calibrate(   data,   variables,   population,   weights = NULL,   method = c("linear", "logit"),   type = c("prop", "count"),   control = list(maxit = 50, epsilon = 1e-07) )

title: Calibrate survey weights to known population totals — calibrate
text: data data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. survey_replicate → error. class → error. variables <tidy-select> Columns calibrate . Must categorical (character factor). Specify bare name c(var1, var2, ...). population Named list population targets. Names must match column names selected variables. element: named numeric vector c(level = target, ...). type = "prop": values must sum 1.0 (within 1e-6 tolerance). type = "count": values must strictly positive. weights <tidy-select> Weight column name (bare name). NULL → auto-detected weighted_df attribute survey object @variables$weights. plain data.frame weights = NULL, uniform starting weights used output column named ".weight". method Character scalar. "linear" (default): one-step exact GREG calibration (may produce negative weights). "logit": bounded iterative calibration (always positive). type Character scalar. "prop" (default): population values proportions. "count": population values counts. control Named list convergence parameters. Merged defaults list(maxit = 50, epsilon = 1e-7) — omitted keys retain defaults.

title: Calibrate survey weights to known population totals — calibrate
text: data.frame weighted_df input → weighted_df survey_taylor survey_nonprob input → class input (survey_taylor survey_nonprob; class preserved) weight column output contains calibrated weights. history entry operation = "calibration" appended weighting_history.

title: Calibrate survey weights to known population totals — calibrate
code: df <- data.frame(   age_group = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+", "18-34", "35-54"),   sex = c("M", "F", "M", "F", "M"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) pop <- list(   age_group = c("18-34" = 0.30, "35-54" = 0.40, "55+" = 0.30),   sex = c("M" = 0.48, "F" = 0.52) ) result <- calibrate(df, variables = c(age_group, sex), population = pop)

title: Kish's effective sample size — effective_sample_size
text: Computes effective sample size using Kish's formula: $$ESS = \frac{(\sum w)^2}{\sum w^2}$$

title: Kish's effective sample size — effective_sample_size
code: effective_sample_size(x, weights = NULL)

title: Kish's effective sample size — effective_sample_size
text: x data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. weighted_df survey objects, weight column auto-detected. weights Bare name (NSE). Weight column. Auto-detected weighted_df survey objects. Required plain data.frame.

title: Kish's effective sample size — effective_sample_size
text: named numeric scalar: c(n_eff = <value>). name "n_eff" part API contract.

title: Kish's effective sample size — effective_sample_size
code: df <- data.frame(x = 1:5, w = c(1.2, 0.8, 1.5, 0.9, 1.1)) effective_sample_size(df, weights = w) #>   n_eff  #> 4.76378

what: Post-stratify survey weights to known joint population cell totals — poststratify
title: Post-stratify survey weights to known joint population cell totals — poststratify
text: Adjusts survey weights weighted cell counts (proportions) match known population values every joint combination stratification variables. Unlike calibrate() rake(), match marginal totals, poststratify() matches exact cross-tabulation cells single pass.

title: Post-stratify survey weights to known joint population cell totals — poststratify
code: poststratify(   data,   strata,   population,   weights = NULL,   type = c("prop", "count") )

title: Post-stratify survey weights to known joint population cell totals — poststratify
text: data data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. survey_replicate -> error. class -> error. strata <tidy-select> Stratification variables jointly define cells. Specify bare name c(var1, var2, ...). Unlike calibrate() rake(), strata variables may type (character, factor, integer, numeric). population data.frame one column per variable selected strata (column names must match exactly), one column named "target", one row per unique cell combination. type = "count": values target must strictly positive. type = "prop": values target must sum 1.0 (within 1e-6). weights <tidy-select> Weight column name (bare name). NULL -> auto-detected weighted_df attribute survey object @variables$weights. plain data.frame weights = NULL, uniform starting weights used output column named ".weight". type Character scalar. "prop" (default): target values proportions summing 1.0. "count": target values population counts. Consistent calibrate() rake().

title: Post-stratify survey weights to known joint population cell totals — poststratify
text: data.frame weighted_df input -> weighted_df survey_taylor survey_nonprob input -> class input (survey_taylor survey_nonprob; class preserved) weight column output contains post-stratified weights. history entry operation = "poststratify" appended weighting_history.

title: Post-stratify survey weights to known joint population cell totals — poststratify
code: df <- data.frame(   age_group = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+", "18-34", "35-54", "55+"),   sex = c("M", "M", "M", "F", "F", "F"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Proportion targets (default type = "prop") pop_prop <- data.frame(   age_group = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+", "18-34", "35-54", "55+"),   sex = c("M", "M", "M", "F", "F", "F"),   target = c(0.14, 0.18, 0.17, 0.15, 0.19, 0.17) ) result <- poststratify(df, strata = c(age_group, sex), population = pop_prop)  # Count targets (explicit type = "count") pop_count <- data.frame(   age_group = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+", "18-34", "35-54", "55+"),   sex = c("M", "M", "M", "F", "F", "F"),   target = c(14000, 18000, 17000, 15000, 19000, 17000) ) result2 <- poststratify(df, strata = c(age_group, sex),   population = pop_count, type = "count")

what: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
title: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
text: Iterative proportional fitting (raking) adjusts survey weights match multiple marginal population totals simultaneously. Supports two algorithms: "anesrake" method (chi-square variable selection, improvement-based convergence) "survey" method (fixed-order IPF, epsilon-based convergence).

title: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
code: rake(   data,   margins,   weights = NULL,   type = c("prop", "count"),   method = c("anesrake", "survey"),   cap = NULL,   control = list() )

title: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
text: data data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. survey_replicate → error. class → error. margins Named list data frame specifying population margin targets. Format — named list:   element can named numeric vector data frame columns level target (formats can mixed within list). Format B — long data frame columns variable, level, target:   Format B auto-detected converted Format use. converted Format stored weighting history. weights <tidy-select> Weight column name (bare name). NULL → auto-detected weighted_df attribute survey object @variables$weights. plain data.frame weights = NULL, uniform starting weights used output column named ".weight". type Character scalar. "prop" (default): margins values proportions. "count": margins values counts. method Character scalar. "anesrake" (default): chi-square discrepancy variable selection improvement-based convergence, anesrake package. "survey": fixed-order IPF cycling margins, epsilon-based convergence, survey::rake(). cap Numeric NULL. Cap weight ratio w / mean(w). weight exceeding cap × mean(w) set cap × mean(w). Applied per-margin adjustment step (post-hoc). NULL (default) means cap. Applies methods. control Named list algorithm parameters. Merged method-specific defaults — omitted keys retain defaults. method = "anesrake" defaults: maxit = 1000: maximum full sweeps improvement = 0.01: percentage improvement convergence threshold pval = 0.05: chi-square p-value threshold variable selection min_cell_n = 0L: minimum unweighted observations per cell (0 = min) variable_select = "total": chi-square aggregation ranking ("total", "max", "average") method = "survey" defaults: maxit = 100: maximum full sweeps epsilon = 1e-7: maximum relative margin error convergence threshold Passing anesrake-specific keys method = "survey" (vice versa) triggers surveywts_warning_control_param_ignored warning per ignored parameter.

title: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
text: data.frame weighted_df input → weighted_df survey_taylor survey_nonprob input → class input (survey_taylor survey_nonprob; class preserved) weight column output contains raked weights. history entry operation = "raking" appended weighting_history.

title: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
text: method = "anesrake": sweep, variables sorted chi-square discrepancy (controlled control$variable_select). Variables cell control$min_cell_n unweighted observations excluded entirely. Variables chi-square p-value exceeds control$pval skipped sweep. Convergence assessed percentage improvement total chi-square consecutive sweeps. variables pass excluded sweep 1, surveywts_message_already_calibrated message emitted. method = "survey": Variables raked fixed order given margins. variables participate every sweep. Convergence assessed maximum relative error across margin cells falling control$epsilon.

title: Rake survey weights to marginal population totals — rake
code: df <- data.frame(   age_group = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+", "18-34", "35-54"),   sex       = c("M", "F", "M", "F", "M"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) margins <- list(   age_group = c("18-34" = 0.30, "35-54" = 0.40, "55+" = 0.30),   sex       = c("M" = 0.48, "F" = 0.52) ) result <- rake(df, margins = margins)

what: Summarize the distribution of survey weights — summarize_weights
title: Summarize the distribution of survey weights — summarize_weights
text: Returns tibble summary statistics weight column, optionally computed within groups defined .

title: Summarize the distribution of survey weights — summarize_weights
code: summarize_weights(x, weights = NULL, by = NULL)

title: Summarize the distribution of survey weights — summarize_weights
text: x data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. weighted_df survey objects, weight column auto-detected. weights Bare name (NSE). Weight column. Auto-detected weighted_df survey objects. Required plain data.frame. <tidy-select> Optional grouping variables. NULL (default), single-row summary observations returned. specified, one row returned per unique group combination.

title: Summarize the distribution of survey weights — summarize_weights
text: tibble columns n, n_positive, n_zero, mean, cv, min, p25, p50, p75, max, ess. non-NULL, group columns precede summary columns.

title: Summarize the distribution of survey weights — summarize_weights
code: df <- data.frame(   group = c("A", "A", "B", "B"),   w = c(1.2, 0.8, 1.5, 0.9) ) summarize_weights(df, weights = w) #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>       n n_positive n_zero  mean    cv   min   p25   p50   p75   max   ess #>   <int>      <int>  <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     4          4      0   1.1 0.287   0.8 0.875  1.05  1.27   1.5  3.77 summarize_weights(df, weights = w, by = c(group)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 12 #>   group     n n_positive n_zero  mean    cv   min   p25   p50   p75   max   ess #>   <chr> <int>      <int>  <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A         2          2      0   1   0.283   0.8  0.9    1    1.1    1.2  1.92 #> 2 B         2          2      0   1.2 0.354   0.9  1.05   1.2  1.35   1.5  1.88

what: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration — surveywts-package
title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration — surveywts-package
text: Provides tools calibrating survey weights known population totals using GREG, raking (iterative proportional fitting), post-stratification. Supports nonresponse adjustment via weighting-class method, effective sample size diagnostics, full weighting history tracking reproducible survey analysis workflows.

title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration — surveywts-package
text: Calibration: calibrate() — GREG (linear) logit calibration population totals rake() — raking via iterative proportional fitting poststratify() — exact post-stratification cell counts proportions Nonresponse: adjust_nonresponse() — weighting-class nonresponse adjustment Diagnostics: effective_sample_size() — Kish effective sample size weight_variability() — coefficient variation weights summarize_weights() — full weight distribution summary table

title: Tools for Survey Weighting and Calibration — surveywts-package
text: Maintainer: Jacob Dennen jdenn0514@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Coefficient of variation of survey weights — weight_variability

text: Computes coefficient variation (CV) weight column: $$CV = \frac{sd(w)}{mean(w)}$$

title: Coefficient of variation of survey weights — weight_variability
code: weight_variability(x, weights = NULL)

title: Coefficient of variation of survey weights — weight_variability
text: x data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob. weighted_df survey objects, weight column auto-detected. weights Bare name (NSE). Weight column. Auto-detected weighted_df survey objects. Required plain data.frame.

title: Coefficient of variation of survey weights — weight_variability
text: named numeric scalar: c(cv = <value>). name "cv" part API contract.

title: Coefficient of variation of survey weights — weight_variability
code: df <- data.frame(x = 1:5, w = c(1.2, 0.8, 1.5, 0.9, 1.1)) weight_variability(df, weights = w) #>        cv  #> 0.2489648

title: surveywts 0.1.1
text: adjust_nonresponse() now returns rows nonrespondent weights set 0, instead dropping nonrespondent rows. preserves design structure variance estimation. Code uses nrow(result) count respondents use sum(result$weight_col > 0) instead. poststratify() now defaults type = "prop", consistent calibrate() rake(). Existing code relies count default add explicit type = "count".

title: surveywts 0.1.1
text: calibrate(), rake(), poststratify() now delegate survey::calibrate(), survey::rake(), anesrake::anesrake(), survey::postStratify() instead vendored algorithm copies. improves numerical correctness maintainability (#16). adjust_nonresponse() response_status argument now resolves via tidyselect::eval_select() instead rlang::as_name(), supporting tidy-select semantics providing clearer error multi-column selection (#15). Input validation calibrate(), rake(), poststratify(), adjust_nonresponse() now uses survey_base inheritance checks instead listing specific class names (#15). summarize_weights() grouped path now uses paste(sep = "//") instead interaction(), avoiding separator collisions factor levels containing dots (e.g., "Dr.") (#13). survey_nonprob print method now shows "Variance: model-assisted (SRS assumption)" instead incorrectly labelling Taylor linearization (#13).

title: surveywts 0.1.1
text: Moved shared helpers .check_input_class() .get_history() R/utils.R; inlined %||% operator (#12). Moved survey Suggests Imports; added anesrake Imports (#16).

title: surveywts 0.1.0
text: adjust_nonresponse() now returns rows nonrespondent weights set 0, instead dropping nonrespondent rows. preserves design structure variance estimation. Code uses nrow(result) count respondents use sum(result$weight_col > 0) instead. poststratify() now defaults type = "prop", consistent calibrate() rake(). Existing code relies count default add explicit type = "count".

title: surveywts 0.1.0
text: first release surveywts, implementing core survey weighting workflow.

title: surveywts 0.1.0
text: weighted_df: S3 subclass tibble carries weight_col attribute identifying weight column weighting_history attribute recording every weighting operation applied. Produced output calibration nonresponse functions input plain data.frame weighted_df. Supports dplyr verbs (select(), rename(), mutate()) automatic downgrade plain tibble (warning) weight column removed. survey_nonprob (surveycore): surveywts implements print() survey_nonprob objects, displaying design variables weighting history.

title: surveywts 0.1.0
text: calibrate(): Calibrate survey weights known marginal population totals using linear (GREG) logit (bounded IRLS) calibration categorical auxiliary variables. rake(): Iterative proportional fitting marginal population targets. Supports two methods: "anesrake" (chi-square variable selection improvement-based convergence) "survey" (fixed-order IPF epsilon-based convergence). Margins may named list long data frame variable, level, target columns. poststratify(): Exact post-stratification known joint population cell counts proportions single non-iterative pass. adjust_nonresponse(): Weighting-class nonresponse adjustment redistributes nonrespondent weights respondents within cells defined . Methods "propensity" "propensity-cell" stubbed Phase 2. effective_sample_size(): Kish’s effective sample size (ESS = (Σw)² / Σw²). weight_variability(): Coefficient variation survey weights. summarize_weights(): Full distributional summary (n, mean, CV, ESS, percentiles), optionally grouped one variables. functions accept data.frame, weighted_df, survey_taylor, survey_nonprob inputs, append structured weighting history entry every call.

title: surveywts 0.1.0
text: calibrate(), rake(), poststratify() now preserve input class (survey_taylor survey_nonprob) rather promoting survey object inputs survey_nonprob (#10).
